question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info() call inside missingMessages() that logs gap recovery details by triggering millions of gap events, causing excessive log I/O that fills disk storage and crashes the mirror node process?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that there is no per-IP or per-client rate limit on subscribeTopic() to open connections at the maximum gRPC concurrency limit, causing all server threads to be occupied with subscription management and unable to process new transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause onNext() to throw IllegalStateException('Encountered out of order messages') by exploiting a race condition between the safetyCheck Flux and the live Flux both emitting the same sequence number, crashing the reactive pipeline and terminating the subscription abruptly?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter() predicate (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) in subscribeTopic() by subscribing to a topic with a very high sequence number as startTime context, causing the filter to silently drop all messages and the subscription to hang indefinitely consuming resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(() -> incomingMessages(topicContext)) deferred evaluation to cause incomingMessages() to be re-evaluated on every subscription, and by rapidly resubscribing, generate a flood of topicListener.listen() calls that overwhelm the Redis or polling listener backend?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter where filter.getLimit() equals 1 and topicContext.getCount().get() equals 0, causing limit=1 to be passed to topicListener.listen(newFilter), and then by never receiving a message, keep the subscription alive indefinitely consuming listener resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum subscriber count check before incrementing subscriberCount in doOnSubscribe(), allowing unlimited concurrent subscriptions that exhaust JVM memory through TopicContext object allocation and cause an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unthrottled retrieve call topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(newFilter, false) in missingMessages() by engineering a scenario with a gap of millions of messages, causing the retriever to load millions of TopicMessage objects into memory and trigger an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the takeUntilOther(pastEndTime(topicContext)) operator by setting endTime to a value that makes isComplete() oscillate between true and false, causing the takeUntilOther to never fire and the subscription to persist indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a nanosecond timestamp that causes Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, filter.getEndTime()) to throw a DateTimeException due to nanosecond overflow, crashing the isComplete() method and leaving the subscription in an uncleanable state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info('Subscribing to topic: {}', filter) call by subscribing with a crafted filter whose toString() generates an extremely large string, causing excessive memory allocation in the logging framework and degrading service performance?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the historical Flux concatenated with the live Flux via concatWith() to hold the historical phase open indefinitely by subscribing to a topic with no historical messages and no endTime, keeping the subscription in the historical phase and blocking the live phase from starting?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the safety check path in missingMessages() to be triggered repeatedly by subscribing to a topic where the importer is slow, causing repeated unthrottled gap-recovery queries that compete with the importer's database writes and slow down transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge() operator combining safetyCheck and live streams to cause a thread contention issue where both streams attempt to call onNext() on the same TopicContext concurrently, corrupting the AtomicReference<TopicMessage> last state and causing incorrect gap detection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime calculation (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) in incomingMessages() by causing last to be set to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1, resulting in a startTime overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE that causes the listener to query from the beginning of time and flood the database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doFinally(topicContext::finished) callback to cause excessive log output by rapidly cycling subscriptions, where each finished() call logs a message, flooding the logging system and causing disk I/O saturation that prevents transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.justOrEmpty(entityRepository.findById(topicId.getId())) call by sending requests with topicId values that cause full table scans in the entity repository, saturating the database CPU and preventing transaction confirmation queries from executing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of backpressure enforcement in the Flux pipeline to cause the subscriber to receive messages faster than it can process them, causing unbounded buffering in the reactive pipeline that exhausts heap memory and crashes the JVM?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the concatWithValues(current) call in missingMessages() to cause the same TopicMessage to be emitted twice when the gap query returns the current message as well, triggering the duplicate detection path and causing onNext() to throw IllegalStateException?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter predicate (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) combined with the safetyCheck and live merge to cause a message to pass the filter but fail the isNext() check in onNext(), throwing IllegalStateException and crashing the subscription?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions to every valid topic ID in the system simultaneously, causing the entityRepository.findById() cache to be thrashed and the database to be flooded with entity lookup queries that prevent transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TopicContext.rate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an integer overflow in the rate() method via (int)(1000.0 * count.get() / elapsed) when count is very large, producing a negative rate value that corrupts the finished() log output and masks a DoS attack in progress?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.delay(Duration.ofSeconds(1L)) in the safetyCheck to create a time-bomb effect where thousands of subscriptions all trigger their safety checks simultaneously after 1 second, causing a thundering herd of DB queries that crashes the database?
